summary: Bug fix verification PASSED. The critical iteration-1 bug (Alert.alert with multiple buttons being a no-op on react-native-web) has been resolved by replacing it with cross-platform <Modal> components. Full end-to-end on web preview at mobile viewport (390x844): Login -> Emergency screen -> all 4 emergency types (SOS, MEDIS, KRIMINAL, KEBAKARAN) -> confirmation Modal appears -> Send -> POST /api/emergency/alert returns 200 -> Result Modal with OK button -> dismiss. Cancel flow on KRIMINAL also verified (modal closes, no POST). 4/4 POSTs succeeded with HTTP 200. All required testIDs render correctly as data-testid in the DOM: sos-button, emergency-medis-button, emergency-kriminal-button, emergency-kebakaran-button, mode-toggle-button, user-pill, login-email-input, login-password-input, login-submit-button, confirm-cancel-button, confirm-send-button, result-ok-button. NOTE: Initial test run failed because Expo bundle was stale (no testIDs in DOM); after `supervisorctl restart expo` the new bundle served correctly.

issues: Console still warns: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow"' (cosmetic only, no functional impact).
Expo log shows 'Font file for ionicons is empty' error but icons still render via fallback. Should be investigated for production build.

action_items: (Optional/non-blocking) Replace deprecated shadow* style props with boxShadow on web platform branches.
(Optional/non-blocking) Investigate the empty Ionicons font file warning - may impact native builds.
critical_code_review_comments: Bug fix is well-implemented: <Modal> + state-driven confirmAlert/resultAlert is the correct cross-platform pattern.
Confirm modal includes both Batal/Cancel and Kirim/Send buttons with proper bilingual labels (id/en) tied to user.language.
Result modal correctly picks success vs failure icon by title content (Berhasil/Success).
All required testIDs are present and rendered in DOM.

success_rate.backend: 100% (18/18 from iteration 1, not retested as instructed)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on emergency alert e2e flow (4/4 alert types POSTed successfully, cancel flow verified)

seed_data_creation: Reused existing user budi27106@test.com / password123 from iteration 1.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix verified end-to-end. Emergency alert flow on web preview is fully functional. Confirm/result Modal pattern works for all 4 alert types and POST /api/emergency/alert returns 200. testIDs are all properly wired. If future testing fails to find testIDs, restart expo (`sudo supervisorctl restart expo`) to clear stale bundle cache before reporting issues. Test user budi27106@test.com / password123 still exists in DB.
rca_of_issue: Original RCA from iteration 1 was correct: react-native-web's Alert polyfill ignores the buttons array. Fix replaces Alert.alert with a controlled <Modal> using two state vars (confirmAlert, resultAlert). Verified: clicking any of SOS/MEDIS/KRIMINAL/KEBAKARAN now opens the confirm Modal, Kirim/Send button POSTs the alert and on 200 opens the result Modal which can be dismissed via the result-ok-button. Cancel/Batal correctly dismisses the confirm Modal without making a network call. No regressions observed.
